intro: Leonardoxa africana, commonly known as the African ironwood, is a deciduous tree native to tropical regions of West and Central Africa. The extracts of this plant have been traditionally used for various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of fever, pain, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Leonardoxa africana include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include teas, decoctions, and infusions, which are often used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions.
